editor.rationale: Reviewers support publication but want clarifications on reporting completeness and residual uncertainty.

concerns: Evidence base is complete but small (k=1 exhausts the full trial universe); heterogeneity and publication-bias diagnostics are structurally underpowered. Treat as a boundary condition; flag for living-review update when new trials accrue.
Prediction interval not definable for k<3; flag as boundary condition.
RoB 2.0: 1 high-risk + 0 some-concerns domains; add low-RoB sensitivity analysis.
Pooled 1.01 sits outside published benchmark range (corridor 0.72-0.84); closest Jhund 2022 HR 0.78 [0.72, 0.84]. Discussion should reconcile.
k<3 - NNT ≈ 1593 (computed from current trial pool); cannot project to future populations without k>=3 (prediction interval undefined). Interpret MCID against trial-specific populations only.
DL estimator under-estimates τ² for k<10; add REML/Paule-Mandel sensitivity.
QoL / PROs reported in 1 trial(s) (FACT (generic)) but not in headline pool; consider adding as a secondary analysis rather than omitting.
